posts.title: Clearing DNS Cache on Windows/Linux/macOS
posts.description: When DNS changes fail to appear, flush the cache on Windows, Linux, and macOS with ipconfig, resolvectl, and mDNSResponder commands, and check TTL and browser caches to diagnose propagation delays quickly.

posts.title: Setting up multiple Git accounts ssh config alias vs gitconfig includeIf
posts.description: This guide explains how to route SSH keys with ~/.ssh/config aliases and how to split commit author settings per folder with ~/.gitconfig includeIf in a multi-account Git setup. Use it to reduce account mix-ups and authentication mistakes.

posts.title: Supporting Multiple Languages on a Hugo Site
posts.description: Optimize a Hugo multilingual blog by configuring baseURL, sitemap, robots, JSON-LD, Open Graph, and meta descriptions, then prevent duplicate issues with hreflang and canonical tags plus slug and translationKey troubleshooting.

posts.title: How to fix missing TortoiseGit icon overlays on Windows
posts.description: When Windows Explorer hides TortoiseGit status icons, check the ShellIconOverlayIdentifiers limit and priority. Clean up the registry and restart Explorer to bring the overlays back instantly.

posts.title: How to migrate SVN to Git: git svn clone
posts.description: A quick guide to using git svn clone when migrating an SVN repository to Git. Learn how to prepare an authors file, what the -s (std-layout) option means, and which install links and verification commands to use so you can start the migration right away.

posts.title: HTTPS TLS/SSL Free vs Paid Certificates
posts.description: Compare TLS/SSL certificate issuance flows across free (ACME/Let's Encrypt), paid, and cloud-managed options, and design renewal automation plus termination point minimization to reduce cost and operational risk.

posts.title: Recovering a deleted Git branch
posts.description: When you remove a Git branch, use reflog to grab the commit hash, recreate the branch at that point with checkout -b, and confirm the branch and commit state in seconds.

posts.title: Encrypting .env Files with GPG for Safe Commits
posts.description: When you must commit a .env file, encrypt it with GPG to block secret leaks: set up .gitignore rules, run gpg encrypt/decrypt commands, and define a default recipient so the repository stays secure.

posts.title: Setting Up OpenClaw
posts.description: Install the OpenClaw AI agent framework locally, walk through the onboard flow to configure models, channels, and skills, and follow the Telegram bot authentication and security checkpoints to run agents safely.
